title: Community Sundays — $40 Two-Course Sunday Roast

title: Meet the Maker — Amy Farnsworth, Amoise
desc: Hosted wine tasting with winemaker Amy Farnsworth from Amoise, Hawke's Bay low-intervention organic wines. $45pp wine, optional $35pp matching bites. 60-minute seated tasting.
source_quote: $45 p/p for the wine, and an optional $35 p/p for the matching bites.

title: Staycation High Tea — Sunday Session
desc: Seasonal High Tea at Zephyr celebrating Wellington brands including Fix and Fogg, Zany Zeus and Poppies Martinborough. Assortment of sandwiches, warm scones and sweet delicacies with organic loose leaf teas. Optional Prosecco or cocktail add-on. $62pp, children $30pp. Bookings essential.
source_quote: Saturday 11th & Sunday 12th July... $62 per person. Childrens Menu available at $30 per person.

title: Pōneke Shanty Club (1st & 3rd Thursday monthly)
desc: Sea shanty sing-along on the 1st and 3rd Thursday of every month. Music provided. All skill levels welcome. 7–9pm.
source_quote: Join us for two hours of sea shanty sing-along. Music provided. All skill and experience levels welcome!

title: Singing Session (2nd Monday monthly)
desc: Relaxed, friendly unaccompanied traditional singing session on the 2nd Monday of each month. Take turns leading a song or just join in the choruses. 7–9pm.
source_quote: A relaxed, friendly way to get into unaccompanied singing the traditional way, sitting around a table with a pint.

title: Ocean Feast (WOAP)
desc: Wellington on a Plate seafood boil dinner. Three-course share-style seafood dinner with a glass of Prosecco on arrival. Ticketed event celebrating fresh NZ seafood. Thu 13 August, 5:30pm.
source_quote: Ocean Feast. Thursday, 13 & 20 August | 5.30 pm. Tickets include 3 courses and a glass of bubbles on arrival.

title: Ocean Feast (WOAP)
desc: Wellington on a Plate seafood boil dinner. Three-course share-style seafood dinner with a glass of Prosecco on arrival. Ticketed event celebrating fresh NZ seafood. Thu 20 August, 5:30pm.
source_quote: Ocean Feast. Thursday, 13 & 20 August | 5.30 pm. Tickets include 3 courses and a glass of bubbles on arrival.

title: Kumutoto Kai me te Kōrero (WOAP hīkoi)
desc: Wellington on a Plate guided hīkoi tracing the Kumutoto Awa from Kelburn to the waterfront. Starts at Graze with a light lunch and cocktail (collaboration between chef Max Gordy and forager Milan Magan). Walk continues to the visible stretch of the stream for soup, then to Berts bar for a final cocktail. ~2km route. Optional foraging workshop Sat 1 Aug 9-10am. All food and drinks included in ticket price. Ticketed via WOAP website.
source_quote: On Sunday 2nd & Sunday 30th of August, we'll be teaming up with the amazing people behind @kumutotorestorationproject ... starts with a light lunch and cocktail at Graze in Kelburn.

title: Kumutoto Kai me te Kōrero (WOAP hīkoi)
desc: Wellington on a Plate guided hīkoi tracing the Kumutoto Awa from Kelburn to the waterfront. Second date. Starts at Graze with a light lunch and cocktail. ~2km route. Optional foraging workshop Sat 29 Aug 9-10am. All food and drinks included in ticket price. Ticketed via WOAP website.
source_quote: On Sunday 2nd & Sunday 30th of August, we'll be teaming up with the amazing people behind @kumutotorestorationproject

title: From the Fisher's Mouth — Butterfish Degustation (WOAP)
desc: Wellington on a Plate 9-course butterfish degustation with spear-fishermen Tom and Cole of Strait Speared. Each course uses a different part of the butterfish (fillets, collars, roe). Fishermen join the table to discuss sustainable harvesting. Welcome drink included; additional alcohol purchasable. Ticketed via WOAP website.
source_quote: On Monday the 31st of August ... A nine-course degustation using all the parts of the butterfish ... Your ticket includes nine courses and one welcome drink on arrival.

title: Sunday Ink Sessions
desc: Flash tattoos at Rosie's — $40 cash per tattoo. First come first served, 18+ for tattoos. Temp tatts for kids. All-day DJ. Last Sunday of every month, 2-6pm.
source_quote: Flash tattoo for just $40 cash. Last Sunday of every month at Rosie's. Tattoo sittings start at 2pm and run until 6pm.

Glass of Marlborough organic wine included. $76+BF pp. Note: cannot accommodate dietary restrictions; contains garlic, chilli, gluten, dairy, shellfish.

address: Wellington Waterfront (Frank Kitts Park / Whairepo Lagoon), Wellington
title: Matariki Ahi Kā
desc: The waterfront transforms into an immersive trail of fire, light and projection, with performances and a nightly remembrance ceremony. On Fri–Sun 10–12 July 2026, 6pm–9pm nightly. Free, all ages.
source_quote: Matariki Ahi Kā — the waterfront glows with fire, light and a nightly remembrance ceremony. Wellington Waterfront, Fri-Sun 6pm-9pm nightly. Free.

address: Porirua Harbour, 2 Wi Neera Drive, Porirua
title: Harbourside Light Display
desc: A free walk-through outdoor light display along the Porirua waterfront, with food trucks on hand. Thu–Sun 5:30pm–9pm.
source_quote: Light Display at the Harbourside — a free walk-through outdoor light display along the Porirua waterfront, with food trucks on hand. Thu-Sun 5:30pm-9pm. Free.

title: LEGO Star Wars: The Exhibition
desc: Over 8 million LEGO bricks bring the galaxy to life at Tākina, plus hands-on building activities for all ages. Timed sessions daily through the July school holidays (to ~19 July 2026). Adult $35, child $22.50, family $105.
source_quote: LEGO Star Wars: The Exhibition — over 8 million LEGO bricks bring the galaxy to life at Tākina. Timed sessions daily.

title: Wellington Ice Rink
desc: The pop-up waterfront ice rink is open daily 10am–10pm through the July school holidays (to ~19 July 2026). Adult $26, child (5–12) $21, 3–4yr $16, family $70. Min age 3, helmet or approved headband required.
source_quote: Wellington Ice Rink — the pop-up waterfront ice rink is back and open daily 10am-10pm through the school holidays.

title: The Lock Inn
desc: A one-night-only lock-in event reviving the spirit of Wellington's old after-hours drinking culture, part of Wellington On a Plate.
source_quote: Thistle Inn, THE LOCK INN, Sat 1 Aug, 6:30pm - Late

title: French Cheese Escape
desc: Guided fromage-flight tasting through four French cheeses with a tasting passport and region guide, running Thursdays to Saturdays through August as part of Wellington On a Plate.
source_quote: Le Marché Français, FRENCH CHEESE ESCAPE, Every Thu–Fri, 1pm - 1:30pm & 2pm - 2:30pm & 3pm - 3:30pm; Every Sat, 10am - 10:30am & 11am - 11:30am & 12pm - 12:30pm & 1pm - 1:30pm

title: Hidden Ferments
desc: Chefs from Chaat Street and Melbourne's Saadi restaurant collaborate on a five-course tasting menu exploring Indian fermentation traditions, part of Wellington On a Plate.
source_quote: Chaat Street, HIDDEN FERMENTS, Sat 29 Aug, Sun 30 Aug, 7pm - Late

title: Royal New Zealand Ballet presents Winter Season
desc: RNZB's Winter Season runs three performances at the St James Theatre.
source_quote: Wellington. 16–18 Jul 2026. St James Theatre. Thu 16 Jul 2026 7:30 pm

title: Mediterranea
desc: A Mediterranean dinner tracing the owner's childhood on a Mediterranean island, from Italy to Spain, Greece and the Middle East. Part of Wellington On a Plate.
source_quote: Cicio Cacio Osteria, MEDITERRANEA, Sun 23 Aug, Mon 24 Aug, Sun 30 Aug, Mon 31 Aug, 5pm - 9:30pm

title: School Holiday Winter Carnival
desc: Two weeks of free carnival entertainment for the school holidays, including bumper cars, face painting, balloon twisting, a hula hoop show, and a Pokemon Trade Day on 16 July.
source_quote: School Holiday Winter Carnival at Johnsonville Shopping Centre! ... two exciting weeks of carnival-themed entertainment ... from 6–17 July 2026
